description:
You are a Sustainability Engineer designing an eco-friendly adobe house in Albuquerque, NM. The starting project 'building-passive-heating.ng3' is open in Energy3D. Your goal is to compare the winter daily heating load of standard wood-frame construction vs. high-thermal-mass adobe construction.

First, change the project location to Albuquerque, NM (or latitude ~35°N) and set the simulation date to January 21. Run a 'Daily Building Energy Analysis' on the baseline house, and export the analysis data to a CSV file named '~/Documents/Energy3D/baseline_wood_frame.csv'.

Next, modify all walls of the house to simulate thick adobe materials: set the Wall U-value to exactly 0.40 W/m²·℃ and set the Wall Volumetric Heat Capacity to exactly 0.5 kWh/m³·℃. Re-run the 'Daily Building Energy Analysis' for January 21 with these new parameters, and export the new analysis data to a CSV file named '~/Documents/Energy3D/adobe_thermal_mass.csv'.

Finally, save the modified 3D model as '~/Documents/Energy3D/adobe_house_design.ng3'.
